evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated, continuously-collected network and IAM configuration showing strict environment boundaries and zero cross-environment ACLs; continuous access logs showing no unauthorized dev→prod accesses; data-copy logs showing masked or no production data in non-prod; up-to-date exception register with short-lived approvals.
Weak: point-in-time architecture diagrams or annual attestations; manual screenshots of configs; lack of logging for cross-environment access; exceptions without expiry or approvals.

control_type_classification.reasoning: "shall be separated and secured" mandates preventive separation to stop breaches or misuse before they occur.

TDA-41.differentiation_note: This control specifically requires measurement of environment segregation and isolation coverage (network/IAM/data separation and exceptions), unlike sibling controls that might focus on secure development practices, encryption, or vulnerability remediation; the unique need is measuring boundary enforcement and presence of production data in non-prod.

TDA-42.measurement_goal: Measure coverage and compliance of test information selection, protection and lifecycle actions: percentage of test datasets that are approved/ classified and have required protections (masking/encryption/RBAC) applied, count of test datasets derived from production without anonymization, and rate of timely retention/deletion/provisioning actions against policy.
risk_categories: Communications security, Software development and acquisition

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This is a preventive control focused on coverage and policy adherence (selection/protection/management), so percentage metrics (coverage/compliance rate) are primary; counts of offending datasets and rates/timeliness of lifecycle actions are useful secondary measures to show volume and velocity.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Compute the percentage of active test datasets that have an approved selection record and a protection disposition (masked/anonymized or encrypted) per policy; count test datasets originating from production that lack masking; measure time-to-deprovision or time-to-delete against retention rules and number of access violations to test information.
data_signals: test data inventory records (dataset id, environment, source: production|synthetic)
data classification labels/tags attached to test datasets
approval/selection records for test data (requestor, approver, timestamp, justification)
data transformation/masking/anonymization status and technique metadata
encryption-at-rest/in-transit flags for test environments and datasets
access control lists / RBAC assignments and access grant logs for test datasets
provisioning and deprovisioning event logs (timestamps, actor, dataset id)
retention/deletion action logs and retention policy version referenced
incident or access logs showing production-data use in non-production environments
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated test-data inventory and masking/encryption metadata with timestamps and access logs showing enforcement; approvals recorded in an authoritative system; automated provisioning/deprovisioning events.
Weak: manual attestations, ad-hoc spreadsheets or one-off screenshots of masked data; annual or one-time statements without logs.
Strong: zero or near-zero counts of unmasked production-origin datasets in test environments; Weak: occasional sample checks that do not cover all test datasets.

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control language 'shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed' prescribes preventive actions to avoid misuse or exposure of test information.

TDA-42.differentiation_note: This control focuses specifically on the lifecycle and protection of test information (selection, protection, retention/deprovisioning), unlike sibling controls that may address production data, incident response, or vulnerability management; it therefore requires inventory/classification and masking/encryption evidence rather than only detection logs.

THR-13.measurement_goal: Measure the rate and coverage of threat intelligence integration into project planning and operations (percentage of active projects that incorporate threat intelligence inputs), plus counts and timeliness of threat advisories delivered to responsible owners and acted upon.

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This control is primarily about coverage/integration (preventive assurance) so a percentage (coverage of projects using threat intelligence) is most useful; counts of advisories and trends in timeliness complement coverage metrics.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Compute: (1) number of active projects and number of those projects whose project plan or security requirements reference or include threat intelligence artifacts (percentage coverage); (2) count of threat intelligence advisories/IOCs distributed to project owners over a period; (3) mean time from advisory issuance to recorded mitigation action per project. Measurements must include timestamps and project identifiers to allow mapping and SLA comparisons.
data_signals: project plan documents and metadata indicating 'threat intelligence' or 'TI' section with project_id and last_modified timestamp
record of threat intelligence reports/advisories published (report_id, publish_timestamp, title)
distribution logs or ticketing records showing advisory recipients and timestamps (advisory_id, project_id, recipient, delivery_timestamp)
IOC/indicator ingestion events in security tools (IOC id, ingestion_timestamp, mapped_project_id)
mitigation/action tickets linked to advisories (ticket_id, advisory_id, project_id, action_timestamp, action_description)
threat feed subscription health/status (feed_name, last_successful_fetch_timestamp, error_count)
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated mappings between advisories and project IDs, timestamped distribution logs, and ticketed mitigation actions showing linkage and timeliness
Strong: continuous ingestion logs from threat feeds with successful fetch timestamps and automated IOC distribution events
Weak: ad-hoc email threads or one-off attestations without timestamps or linkage to project documentation
Weak: annual manual reviews or attestations that do not show ongoing distribution or actioning of threat intelligence

affinity_keywords: risks are not identified and treated

control_type_classification.reasoning: Threat intelligence integrated into planning prevents security gaps; associated risk text ('Information security is not addressed in project plans') indicates a preventive integration objective.

THR-13.differentiation_note: Unlike detection-focused TI controls, this entry emphasizes measurement of TI integration into project plans and assignment of responsibilities (coverage, distribution and timeliness) rather than raw detection counts or SIEM alerting.

TPM-53.measurement_goal: Measure the coverage and operationalization of supplier information-security processes: percent of active suppliers with required security clauses and completed risk assessments, count of suppliers with documented security procedures, and supplier-related security incidents and remediation metrics (counts and MTTR).

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This is a preventive control requiring coverage and policy implementation; auditors primarily need coverage percentages (e.g., percent of suppliers with contracts/assessments). Supplement with counts of assessments and rates/trends of supplier incidents to show operationalization over time.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Percentage of active suppliers with an executed contract containing required information-security clauses and a completed supplier risk assessment within the defined policy period; count and rate of supplier-related security incidents in the past 12 months and mean time to remediate incidents attributable to suppliers. Also measure presence of supplier-specific procedures (onboarding, access provisioning, monitoring) as binary/compliance flags per supplier.
data_signals: supplier inventory records (supplier_id, service/product description, criticality classification)
signed contracts / SLA documents metadata indicating presence of information-security clauses and effective/expiry dates
supplier risk assessment records (assessment_date, assessor, risk_rating/severity)
third-party security questionnaires / attestation responses with timestamps
access provisioning logs showing supplier accounts, privileges, last_activity_timestamp
supplier-related incident records (incident_id, supplier_id, breach_flag, detection_time, remediation_time)
onboarding/offboarding checklists and evidence of controls applied (MFA enabled, network segmentation, approved access)
vulnerability/patch records referencing supplier components (vuln_id, asset_id, remediation_date)
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated supplier inventory linked to contract metadata and assessment records with timestamps; completed risk assessments and signed attestations stored in a central system.
Strong: audit reports or penetration test results mapped to supplier components and linked remediation evidence.
Weak: ad-hoc spreadsheets of suppliers without timestamps or signed contracts; annual manual attestations with no linkage to incidents or access logs.
Weak: absence of supplier_id linkage between incidents and supplier records, preventing validation of supplier-related breaches.

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control text requires 'Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented', which is inherently preventative (establishing controls before incidents).

TPM-53.differentiation_note: This control focuses specifically on governance and applied processes for supplier relationships (contractual clauses, risk assessments, monitoring) rather than internal incident response or vulnerability scanning; measurement must emphasize supplier coverage and contractual/assessment evidence rather than purely technical detection data.

TPM-54.measurement_goal: Measure coverage and currency of ICT supplier security governance: percentage of ICT suppliers (by criticality) with documented security requirements in contracts, with completed supplier risk assessments and remediation tracking; plus count of vendor-related security incidents and projects that include supplier security in project plans.

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This is a preventive control requiring coverage and implementation across suppliers, so percentage metrics (coverage of suppliers with required artifacts) are primary; absolute counts (number of suppliers, incidents) and trends (coverage over time) are useful secondary measures to show adoption and change.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Percentage of ICT suppliers classified as critical that have a signed contract or SOW containing required information security clauses and a completed supplier risk assessment within the policy-defined period (e.g., 12 months). Additionally measure count of supplier-related security incidents and number/percentage of projects that include supplier security requirements in their project plans.
data_signals: third-party supplier inventory records (supplier_id, product/service, criticality classification, last_sync_timestamp)
contract records showing security clause presence/contract_signed_date (contract_id, security_clause_present boolean)
supplier risk assessment reports and assessment_date (assessment_id, risk_rating, assessor_id)
onboarding/offboarding checklist completion timestamps for suppliers (checklist_id, completed_timestamp)
remediation ticket records for supplier-identified issues (ticket_id, vendor_id, severity, opened_timestamp, resolved_timestamp)
project plan metadata indicating inclusion of information security requirements (project_id, security_requirements_present boolean, plan_approval_date)
incident reports linked to vendors (incident_id, vendor_id, breach_flag, incident_date)
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated supplier inventory synchronized with authoritative source and last_sync_timestamp within policy period
Strong: signed contracts/SOWs stored with extractable 'security clause' flag and contract_signed_date
Strong: completed supplier risk assessment reports with risk_rating and assessment_date within required frequency
Weak: undated spreadsheets or manual attestations without timestamps or supporting contract documents
Weak: ad-hoc incident narratives without a vendor_id linkage or remediation ticket timestamps

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control text explicitly states 'Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented' and lists domain THIRD_PARTY_MANAGEMENT, indicating preventive measures to manage supply-chain risks.

TPM-54.differentiation_note: This control specifically targets ICT supplier/supply-chain governance (contracts, supplier risk assessments and project plan inclusion), rather than internal operational controls — measurement must focus on supplier inventory, contractual security clauses and assessment/remediation records.

TPM-55.measurement_goal: Measure the coverage and recency of security reviews for suppliers, the count and timeliness of supplier service/change events that were evaluated/approved, and the number and impact of supplier-related security incidents tied to monitored supplier practices.

metric_type_preferences.rationale: Detective controls benefit from event counts (reviews performed, change notifications processed, incidents attributed to suppliers) to demonstrate monitoring activity; percentages show coverage (e.g., percent of suppliers reviewed) and trending shows improvements or gaps over time.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Count of suppliers with a security review or assessment in the required period (e.g., last 12 months) grouped by supplier criticality; count of supplier service or contract change notifications received and count completed/approved within the defined review timeframe; count of supplier-attributed security incidents and their remediation timestamps. Also measure percent coverage of suppliers under active monitoring and average time-to-review for supplier changes.
data_signals: master supplier inventory with supplier criticality/tier and unique supplier ID
security assessment/monitoring report timestamps and outcomes per supplier
supplier change/notification log entries with submission timestamp and review completion timestamp
contract version/change log and effective dates (records showing security-related contract changes)
supplier incident reports with attribution flag, incident timestamp and impact classification
remediation/action logs for supplier issues with open/close timestamps
SLA/monitoring performance logs for supplier service delivery (uptime, SLA breaches)
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated supplier inventory and assessment records with timestamps, signed review documents or workflow completion records, and time-stamped change-review logs linked to supplier IDs; continuous monitoring and SLA breach logs that correlate with supplier events.
Weak: ad-hoc, manual spreadsheets or one-off email confirmations of review, missing timestamps, or narrative-only minutes without links to supplier IDs.

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control text requires the organization to 'monitor, review, evaluate' supplier practices, which are detective/oversight activities rather than purely preventive or corrective actions.

TPM-55.differentiation_note: Unlike onboarding or contract-focused third-party controls, this control specifically requires ongoing monitoring and formal change-review processes for supplier security and service delivery; measurement must track review frequency, change-notification handling, and supplier-attributed incidents rather than just contract presence. Note: 'third_party_management' was mapped to governance_risk_compliance/change_management because the exact focus_area is not in the provided list.

TPM-56.measurement_goal: Measure the coverage and frequency of monitoring and formal reviews applied to outsourced system development activities (count of vendor development deliverables reviewed), plus the count and trend of security findings or incidents attributable to vendor-developed artifacts so an auditor can verify ongoing oversight.

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This is a detective control (monitor/review) where counts of reviews, monitoring events, and vendor-attributed incidents are primary evidence; percentages (coverage) and trends (incidents over time or review frequency) are useful secondary perspectives.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Count of outsourced development artifacts (code commits, deliverables, change requests) that received a documented review or monitoring action per period, percentage of vendor deliverables subject to security testing, and the count/time-trend of security incidents or breaches attributed to vendor-developed components. Include timestamps, vendor identifier, reviewer identity, and outcome (findings, remediation required, remediation completed).
data_signals: code review reports or pull request review records tagged with vendor identifier and timestamps
SAST/DAST/SCA scan results for vendor-submitted deliverables (scan timestamp, findings, severity)
change request / deployment records for vendor-submitted code (change id, approver, timestamp)
access provisioning logs for vendor developer accounts (account id, provisioner, approval records, timestamps)
vendor security assessment/attestation documents and their issuance dates
governance/oversight meeting minutes or review records referencing outsourced development activities
incident/issue tickets tagged to vendor or vendor-delivered components (incident id, root cause attribution, breach flags)
remediation/closure timestamps for findings originating from vendor deliverables
evidence_strength_indicators: Strong: automated, continuous logs and scan results tied to vendor identifiers plus documented review records and remediation timestamps (machine-readable, queryable).
Moderate: periodic formal review minutes combined with sporadic scan reports.
Weak: one-off attestations or undated manual notes without timestamps, absent linkage between vendor artifacts and review/testing evidence.

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control text explicitly states 'monitor and review' and is labeled 'type: detective', indicating oversight and detection activities rather than preventive or corrective measures.

TPM-56.differentiation_note: This control is specifically about oversight of outsourced system development activities (reviews, monitoring, vendor-attributed findings) rather than general third-party risk management or contract controls, so measurements must link monitoring/review artifacts directly to vendor development deliverables.

VPM-75.measurement_goal: Measure coverage and recency of obtained technical vulnerability intelligence mapped to the organization's information systems, and the percentage of identified exposures that have documented risk evaluations and corresponding mitigations or patching actions tracked to completion.

metric_type_preferences.rationale: This is a preventive control focused on coverage and evaluation: percentage metrics (e.g., percent of assets covered, percent of exposures evaluated) directly show coverage/compliance; counts and trends supplement with raw volume and improvement over time.

evidence_requirements.what_to_measure: Percent of information system assets with up-to-date vulnerability intelligence (feed ingestion or scan) mapped to them; count of open vulnerabilities by severity and corresponding documented exposure evaluations; percent of evaluated exposures with a tracked mitigation/patch ticket and remediation timestamp.
data_signals: vulnerability intelligence feed ingestion timestamps (e.g., vendor advisories, CVE feed ingestion)
vulnerability scan/assessment reports and execution timestamps
asset inventory records with software/firmware versions and last-known scan timestamp
vulnerability-to-asset mapping table (vulnerability ID -> asset ID)
vulnerability severity scores (e.g., CVSS) and published exploitability indicators
risk/exposure evaluation records (risk assessment entries stating exposure level)
mitigation or patch deployment/change ticket IDs with creation and close timestamps
open vulnerability count by severity and age (days since first observed)
evidence_strength_indicators: Continuous automated ingestion of vendor/CVE feeds and scheduled scans with timestamps mapped to assets (strong)
Automated vulnerability-to-asset mapping and risk assessment records stored in a central system (strong)
Manual, infrequent vulnerability summaries or single annual reports without mappings or timestamps (weak)
Patch/mitigation tickets without linkage to vulnerability IDs or missing timestamps (weak)

control_type_classification.reasoning: The control requires obtaining vulnerability information, evaluating exposure and taking measures — actions intended to prevent exploitation, so it is primarily preventive.

VPM-75.differentiation_note: This control emphasizes obtaining vulnerability intelligence and evaluating organizational exposure (coverage, mapping, risk evaluation) rather than solely measuring remediation velocity or incident response activities; measurements must focus on inventory coverage and exposure assessment.
